title: Psoriasis Treatment Options: 8 Essential Oils to Consider

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Aloin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce redness, itching, and scaling associated with the condition.
Anthraquinones are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, which can soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.
Aloe-emodin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oil is a natural remedy that can help with psoriasis. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches on the skin. The oil is extracted from the aloe vera plant and has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied to the affected skin, it can reduce redness and swelling, making the skin feel cooler and more comfortable. The oil also has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infections that can sometimes occur with psoriasis. Additionally, aloe barbadensis essential oil can help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin, which can be a major symptom of the condition. This oil can be used in a variety of ways, including applying it directly to the affected skin, adding it to a bath, or using it in a cream or ointment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil that helps with psoriasis, you will need some sesame seeds, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass jar. First, dry the sesame seeds completely in a low oven or by spreading them out in the sun. Once the seeds are dry, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a spice grinder. Next, combine the sesame powder with a carrier oil in a 1:5 ratio, so if you use one tablespoon of sesame powder, use five tablespoons of carrier oil. Stir the mixture well and transfer it to a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, to allow the sesame oil to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the sesame oil from the solids. Discard the solids and use the sesame essential oil as needed to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for psoriasis because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and skin cell growth, which are common symptoms of psoriasis.
Kaempferol is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness, swelling, and skin irritation associated with the condition.

Saponins are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, and also have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oil is a natural remedy that can help to soothe and calm the symptoms of psoriasis. The anti-inflammatory properties in this oil reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to manage the condition. It also has antiseptic properties that prevent infections and promote healing of the skin. This oil helps to improve skin texture and reduce scarring, which is beneficial for people with psoriasis who often experience skin thickening and scarring. Calendula officinalis essential oil can also help to reduce the itching and burning sensations associated with psoriasis, allowing people to find relief and comfort. Additionally, it has been shown to improve skin hydration, which is essential for maintaining healthy skin and preventing further psoriasis flare-ups.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil for psoriasis relief, you'll need sesame seeds, a carrier oil, a clean glass container, and a distillation apparatus or a slow cooker. First, dry and clean the sesame seeds, then mix them with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a 1:5 ratio. For example, use one part sesame seeds and five parts carrier oil. Store this mixture in a clean glass container and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container occasionally, allowing the sesame oil to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the sesame seeds. Next, use a distillation apparatus or a slow cooker to extract the essential oil from the infused mixture. If using a distillation apparatus, heat the mixture gently to separate the essential oil from the carrier oil. If using a slow cooker, cook the mixture on low heat for several hours, then let it cool and strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter. Finally, store your Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle, away from direct sunlight and heat. You can apply this oil topically to affected areas, but always dilute it with a carrier oil, as it can be strong.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, promoting healthy skin and alleviating symptoms of the condition.
Quercetin is good for psoriasis because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and skin damage associated with the condition.

Cineole is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling of the skin, and also has antioxidant properties that can help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as tea tree oil, has been used for centuries to treat various skin conditions, including psoriasis. The oil contains powerful compounds like terpinen-4-ol and cineole, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis, promoting a clearer and smoother skin appearance. Tea tree oil also has antifungal properties, which can help control the growth of yeast and fungi that can cause psoriasis flare-ups. When applied topically to the affected skin, tea tree oil can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. Additionally, tea tree oil may help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms by reducing the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil for psoriasis, you will need dried Sesamum indicum seeds. First, dry the seeds completely in a warm, shaded area or using a food dehydrator. Once dry, crush the seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder. Next, extract the oil from the powder by mixing it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:10. Let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it occasionally. After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids. Discard the solids and transfer the extracted oil to a clean glass bottle. Finally, store the Sesamum indicum essential oil in a cool, dark place to preserve its potency and use it topically to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms. However, please note that before using any herbal essential oil, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist to ensure safe and proper use.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, which can ease the symptoms of psoriasis.

Kaempferol is good for psoriasis because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis, and also inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that contribute to the skin condition.
Tannins are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, which can soothe and calm itchy, irritated skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, is a plant that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including skin problems like psoriasis. The essential oil extracted from its leaves has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce redness and itching associated with psoriasis. It also has antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which can prevent infections and promote healing of the skin. The oil's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can further irritate the skin. Additionally, Urtica dioica essential oil has been found to have immunomodulatory effects, meaning it can help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, which is often a major contributor to psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil that helps with psoriasis, start by harvesting the seeds from the Sesame plant (Sesamum indicum) and drying them thoroughly in a shaded area or using a food dehydrator on a low temperature setting. Once the seeds are completely dry, grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine the sesame seed powder with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a ratio of about 1 part sesame seed to 10 parts carrier oil. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of around 100°F to 150°F (38°C to 65°C). After heating the mixture, let it cool and then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. The resulting Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil can be stored in a cool, dark place and used as a topical treatment for psoriasis by applying a few drops to the affected area and gently massaging it into the skin.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce skin redness and irritation, helping to soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.
Ferulic acid is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition.
Saponins are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, and also promote the healing of skin cells.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oil, also known as oat kernel oil, has been used to help soothe and calm skin irritations, including psoriasis. This is because oat kernel oil contains saponins, a type of chemical that helps reduce inflammation and itching. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches on the skin, and the inflammation and itching can be very uncomfortable. Avena sativa oil may help to reduce the severity of these symptoms by creating a barrier on the skin that locks in moisture and soothes the skin. This can help to reduce redness and itching, making it easier to manage the condition. Additionally, oat kernel oil is known to be gentle and non-irritating, making it a good choice for people with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oils for psoriasis relief, start by collecting the seeds of the Sesame plant, also known as Sesamum indicum. The seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin. First, dry the sesame seeds in a warm and dry place for 2-3 days to remove any moisture. Next, grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder. Then, mix 1 cup of the sesame seed powder with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Store the mixture in a cool and dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to allow the oil to infuse with the sesame seed properties. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the sesame seed powder and use the infused oil as a topical treatment for psoriasis, applying a few drops to the affected area and gently massaging it into the skin. This homemade essential oil can help reduce inflammation, soothe the skin, and promote healing, providing relief from psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for psoriasis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for psoriasis because it helps to reduce inflammation and repair damaged skin by blocking the production of molecules that cause skin cell growth and inflammation, which can help to relieve symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, itching, and scaly patches.
Fisetin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, and also promote the growth of new skin cells to replace damaged ones.
Tannins are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, which can ease the symptoms and discomfort of psoriasis.
Saponins are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail essential oil, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help soothe and calm skin conditions like psoriasis. The oil is rich in silica, which helps to strengthen skin cells and improve skin elasticity. This can be especially helpful for people with psoriasis, who often experience skin irritation, redness, and flaking. The anti-inflammatory properties of the oil can also help to reduce swelling and irritation, promoting a more even skin tone. Additionally, the oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help to prevent infections that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. When used topically, Equisetum arvense essential oil can help to moisturize and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of psoriasis plaques and promoting a more balanced skin ecosystem.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil that helps with psoriasis, you will need dried Sesamum indicum leaves or seeds. First, clean and dry the leaves or seeds thoroughly. Then, place them in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Leave the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every day. This process is called infusion, and it allows the beneficial compounds from the Sesamum indicum to mix with the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the solids and store the oil in a dark glass bottle in a cool place. To use the oil for psoriasis, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut oil and apply it to the affected skin area 2-3 times a day. Sesamum indicum essential oil has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the symptoms of psoriasis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oil, also known as St John's Wort oil, is a popular natural remedy for psoriasis. It contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with psoriasis. The oil can also promote skin healing and reduce the risk of infection. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can make psoriasis symptoms worse. When applied topically, the oil can be absorbed into the skin, where it can help to calm and soothe the affected areas, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with psoriasis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil for psoriasis, you'll need Sesamum indicum seeds, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a clean glass container. First, dry the Sesamum indicum seeds in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 2-3 hours, or until they are completely dry and brittle. Then, grind the seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 1 part of the powdered seeds with 5 parts of carrier oil in the glass container. Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container every day to allow the seeds' active compounds to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the Sesamum indicum essential oil to a dark glass bottle and label it for future use. This oil can be applied topically to affected skin areas to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum essential oil is a natural remedy that may help with psoriasis symptoms. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches. Sesamum indicum oil is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the skin. It also has antiseptic properties, which can help prevent infections that may occur due to damaged skin. The oil's moisturizing properties can help soothe and calm dry, itchy skin, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, Sesamum indicum oil may help to promote skin cell regeneration, which can aid in the healing of psoriasis lesions.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sesamum indicum herbal essential oil that helps with psoriasis, you'll need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you'll need to dry the leaves and flowers of the Sesamum indicum plant, which is also known as the African or Indian sesame plant. This can be done by tying the plant in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, and well-ventilated area. Once the plant material is completely dry, you can extract the essential oil by placing the dried leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar and covering them with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day, to allow the oil to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids. This Sesamum indicum essential oil has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, making it a potential remedy for psoriasis symptoms. However, please note that before using any herbal essential oil, especially if you have a skin condition, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist.

intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be beneficial for people with psoriasis, a condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly skin. Psoriasis can be painful and embarrassing, making everyday activities challenging. Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, is often used to soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms. Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing properties keep the skin hydrated and healthy.  Calendula officinalis, or calendula, is another herb that can help with psoriasis. It has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that promote healing and reduce inflammation. Calendula oil can be applied topically to affected areas, helping to calm and soothe the skin. By using these herbal essential oils, people with psoriasis may experience relief from symptoms and improve their quality of life. Managing psoriasis is essential because it can lead to complications like skin infections, joint damage, and even depression. By addressing the condition, individuals can regain confidence, participate in activities they enjoy, and live a more comfortable life.
supplementary_best: For psoriasis, a combination of Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, and Melaleuca alternifolia may be beneficial. Aloe barbadensis helps soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with psoriasis. Calendula officinalis, on the other hand, has antiseptic properties that can help prevent infections that can worsen psoriasis. It also promotes wound healing and tissue repair, which can aid in the recovery of damaged skin. Melaleuca alternifolia, or tea tree oil, has antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections and reduce inflammation. Its antifungal properties can also help control fungal infections that can contribute to psoriasis. This combination of soothing, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis and promote skin healing.
supplementary_avoid: If you have psoriasis, there are some herbal essential oils you should avoid. One of these oils is Tea Tree oil (Melaleuca spp. is mentioned in your exceptions list, but let's talk about the other Melaleuca species) - which can be irritating to the skin and cause more inflammation. Another oil to avoid is Wintergreen oil (Gaultheria procumbens), which contains methyl salicylate, a compound that can make psoriasis symptoms worse. You should also avoid Oregano oil (Origanum spp.), as it can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. In addition, avoid using Peppermint oil (Mentha piperita), as it can cause skin irritation and make your symptoms worse. Another oil to be cautious with is Thyme oil (Thymus spp.), especially the red variety, as it can be irritating to the skin. Finally, avoid using Birch oil (Betula species), as it can cause skin irritation and make your psoriasis symptoms worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help with psoriasis. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling. Aloe vera also has moisturizing properties that can help to hydrate the skin, which is often dry and cracked in people with psoriasis. When applied topically, aloe vera can help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms and promote healthy skin growth.  Bath salts made from Epsom salt and sea salt can also be beneficial for people with psoriasis. Epsom salt is rich in magnesium, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. When added to warm bath water, these salts can help to relieve itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis. The warm water can also help to loosen and remove dead skin cells, which can clog pores and make psoriasis symptoms worse.  Olive oil is another natural remedy that can help with psoriasis. It is rich in antioxidants and fatty acids that can help to moisturize and nourish the skin. When applied topically, olive oil can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin growth. It can also help to lock in moisture and reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms.  Apple cider vinegar can be used as a natural remedy for psoriasis when applied topically. It has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms. When applied to the skin, apple cider vinegar can help to balance the skin's pH and reduce inflammation. However, it's essential to dilute the apple cider vinegar with water before applying it to the skin, as it can be harsh and irritating if used undiluted.  Turmeric, which contains a compound called curcumin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help with psoriasis. When applied topically, turmeric can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin growth. It can also help to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms and promote relaxation.  These natural remedies can help to alleviate psoriasis symptoms by reducing inflammation, promoting healthy skin growth, and moisturizing the skin.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with psoriasis often experience other health issues. They may have arthritis, which causes joint pain and stiffness. A study showed that turmeric, a plant-based anti-inflammatory, can help reduce joint pain. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which blocks chemicals that cause inflammation.  Those with psoriasis are also at risk for heart disease. A condition called atherosclerosis can cause plaque to build up in arteries, leading to heart attacks and strokes. Herbal supplements like ginkgo biloba and hawthorn can help improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, which may reduce the risk of heart disease.  People with psoriasis are also more likely to have anxiety and depression. A study found that omega-3 fatty acids, which are found in fish oil supplements, can help reduce symptoms of anxiety and depression. These fatty acids can also help reduce inflammation in the body.  Psoriasis patients may also experience digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). A study showed that probiotics, which are found in fermented foods like yogurt and kefir, can help reduce symptoms of IBS. Probiotics can also help reduce inflammation in the gut.  Another issue people with psoriasis may experience is high blood sugar levels. A study found that cinnamon, a spice made from a plant, can help lower blood sugar levels. Cinnamon can also help improve insulin sensitivity, which can reduce the risk of developing type 2 diabetes.  These herbal remedies can be used in combination with conventional treatments to help manage symptoms of psoriasis and other related health issues. However, it's essential to talk to a healthcare professional before adding any new supplements to your regimen.
